refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic but slightly vague about the evaluation period and the nature of the availability target. Refining it to explicitly include the evaluation period and clarifying that the availability target is customer-defined makes it clearer and still solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the teleoperation link availability over the agreed evaluation period and verify that it meets or exceeds the customer-defined target (e.g., ≥ 0.999).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant communication paths to ensure failover capability
Incorporate real-time link health monitoring and adaptive error correction
Design a robust link management protocol with automatic recovery and reconnection
Utilize predictive analytics to anticipate and mitigate link degradation
Establish a modular communication architecture allowing dynamic reconfiguration
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant communication paths to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Redundancy mechanisms may interact with link management protocols if not properly isolated
coupling_check.notes: Selecting redundancy as the primary DP supports independence by isolating failure modes and enabling failover without tight coupling to other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that redundancy can be implemented within operational policies and safety plans
Risk that redundancy increases system complexity and cost
Assumes that failover mechanisms do not introduce unacceptable latency impacting teleoperation
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including link management and monitoring as secondary DPs to complement redundancy and enhance overall availability robustness.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall network and communication infrastructure responsible for managing communication paths and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles the physical and logical network connections used for communication redundancy and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends status and health data about communication paths and failover status to other modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as redundant communication paths are core to network management; minimal diffusion as failover logic is centralized here.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not manage redundancy; would require diffusion of failover logic across vehicle components.
Teleoperation depends on communication but does not control redundancy; would cause significant diffusion if responsible.
Safety Services may monitor communication health but does not implement redundancy; partial containment but moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of implementing redundant communication paths, as it directly manages network infrastructure and failover capabilities, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on external network hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
Coordination with Safety Services and Teleoperation modules may require well-defined interfaces to avoid cross-module diffusion.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision needed; the DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module boundary, ensuring good containment and minimal diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing redundant communication paths directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target by ensuring failover capability, which is essential to meet or exceed the required link availability of ≥ 0.999 over the evaluation period.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on external network hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
Coordination with Safety Services and Teleoperation modules may require well-defined interfaces to avoid cross-module diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and minimal diffusion for the DP, as it centralizes the failover logic and manages network infrastructure. Other modules have lower containment scores and higher diffusion risks, confirming NGHE Solution as the appropriate IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring the measured link availability against the customer-defined target and evaluating telemetry data on communication path status and failover events provided by the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and well-justified, with the module responsible for managing redundant communication paths and failover mechanisms, ensuring ownership clarity.

recommended_revisions: No revision needed; the DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module boundary, ensuring good containment and minimal diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and no recommended revisions. The DP of implementing redundant communication paths effectively meets the FR for teleoperation link availability, and the NGHE Solution module is an appropriate IM boundary with high cohesion and minimal diffusion. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and no further decomposition is necessary at this high safety criticality level.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can fully encapsulate the failover logic and manage network infrastructure dependencies.
Interfaces with Safety Services and Teleoperation modules will be well-defined to avoid diffusion.
open_risks: Potential dependency on external network hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
Coordination with Safety Services and Teleoperation modules may require careful interface management to prevent cross-module diffusion.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data on communication path status and failover events to verify link availability.
Monitoring results demonstrating achievement of the customer-defined link availability target (≥ 0.999).
